title: New vs old
wikitext:
[]: New vs old             Quote Post by amandaknox » Sun Sep 28, 2014 8:33 am I'm a big fan of the original series, but also like some of the touches added to the revamped series. There's good bits in both. The original series has a lot of unnecessary scenes while the newer one is missing some good scenes and I'm not keen on the direction they go with the 3rd movie. What are your thoughts?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Sep 28, 2014 8:42 am Some background reading thread/11561/Debate-the-quality-of-Rebuild-here-1/ thread/7773/Debate-the-quality-of-Rebuild-here-2/ Personally, I felt that Jo slid off into the Pit of Voles after Operation Yashima. But then I'm increasingly an OA purist, too.

[]:             Quote Post by amandaknox » Sun Sep 28, 2014 8:49 am I don't know if everyone has seen 3.33 already, so I won't give anything away, but I liked that they put time into getting to know Kaworu, other than that I don't like the circumstances of that whole movie, it went a weird direction. I'm kind of feeling the need to download everything Evangelion ever made and merge the best of everything together, so many great scenes from the original missing.

[]: Re: New vs old             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Sep 28, 2014 8:58 am amandaknox wrote: unnecessary scenes

[]:             Quote Post by amandaknox » Sun Sep 28, 2014 8:59 am Scenery panning mostly, also some of the soul searching didn't need so much. I also preferred how Asuka was introduced originally as opposed to the way they did it in the new one

[]:             Quote Post by ThanatosII » Wed Oct 01, 2014 8:37 am I believe the pitfall of NTE is their flauntness to add unnecessary stuff to replace things performed better in the originals. The most notable example being when the Dummy System activates in Ha, Khara somehow believed it would be better to have Shinji look at a red star field. It simply gave less credence to Shinji's pleas to stop the madness. In episode 18, he was a witness to it all and you could feel the connection between the pilot and the rawness of the situation. I really never bought Shinji knowing or 'sensing' that Unit 01 was about to chomp down on the entry plug in Ha since too many things were being smashed and flown around already. The very few things that I will praise NTE for is adding more intensity to some Angels (excluding Unit 03). Certain fights like Ramiel and Sahaquiel just look a little too cheesy in the originals. Specifically Sahaquiel, as it made more sense for Gendo to praise Shinji given the fact his Unit did the most work and took the most damage. In the originals, seeing how all 3 pilots returned safely to the bridge of NERV HQ, Gendo's praise seems pretty random now, or rather, holds less weight compared to the circumstances of NTE. Obviously overall, the originals will have all the necessary scenes and the movies have no choice but to leave out things due to runtime.

[]:             Quote Post by Rosenakahara » Wed Oct 01, 2014 9:40 am im going to get hung for this but.....i like rebuild more than the series *flame shield up* EoE is better than all but i actually really do prefer the way rebuild did it, this is in no way implying i do not love the OG series because i do, its awesome however i just personally love the direction its going in with 3.33 and while i did love the character discussion and regression in the series i preferred the development in rebuild, then again this may be because i watched rebuild 1 and 2 first then hopped onto the series, i got a bit more out of watching them back 2 back (it's spinning on - Back!) I would also like to say i don't really think there were any unnecessary scenes in either series, oh also i actually think the changed bardiel scene was there for a reason, it is somewhat foreshadowing what happens at the end of 2.0, with bardiel Shinji refuses to act and destroy bardiel for fear of hurting Asuka, as a result his sight and control is taken away from him and destruction all around ensues, in the end of 2.0 Shinji does actually act but he turns a blind eye to everyone else only focusing on Rei, he almost destroys the entire world because he chooses to be blind to the destruction around him, that really isnt much to go on but still its what i came up with when watching

[]:             Quote Post by ThanatosII » Wed Oct 01, 2014 9:58 am oh also i actually think the changed bardiel scene was there for a reason, it is somewhat foreshadowing what happens at the end of 2.0, with bardiel Shinji refuses to act and destroy bardiel for fear of hurting Asuka, as a result his sight and control is taken away from him and destruction all around ensues, in the end of 2.0 Shinji does actually act but he turns a blind eye to everyone else only focusing on Rei, he almost destroys the entire world because he chooses to be blind to the destruction around him, that really isnt much to go on but still its what i came up with when watching

[]:             Quote Post by amandaknox » Wed Oct 01, 2014 10:24 am I like the build up of 1.11 and 2.22, Shinji came across as more of a realistic teenage boy, but 3.33 lost me, I'd prefer for it to be replaced with the episode with Kaworu then EoE, but I'd probably nick a few scenes from 3.33 to add in because at least 3.33 had a build up of them getting to know each other, it's better for them to have more than 5 minutes on screen together before the writers try to pass them off as close friends.

[]:             Quote Post by Merkaba » Wed Oct 01, 2014 10:25 am Rosenakahara wrote: stuff

[]:             Quote Post by ThanatosII » Wed Oct 01, 2014 11:25 am Boils down to what you look for in the story of Evangelion. I wanted to find something (well not intentionally at the time) with relatable characters beyond a little quirk (i.e. "Oh I totally see myself in him/her because he/she is an outcast!"). The way NGE thoroughly examines the main characters is something I have yet to see done in any other visual medium. Characters that establish such connection, to me, a viewer, is very profound. In this respect, I could hardly say Rebuild overall is superior. However, I can acknowledge someone wants something different out of an Eva story, and Rebuilds surely do that. Just curious what that is though.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Wed Oct 01, 2014 12:50 pm Hey, no shame in preferring NTE or the manga over the show. Different people like different things.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Oct 01, 2014 1:51 pm Merkaba wrote: Kudos for having the balls to say it

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Wed Oct 01, 2014 5:08 pm Reichu wrote: "Balls", eh? Might want to rethink that choice of word

[]:             Quote Post by KingXanaduu » Wed Oct 01, 2014 5:32 pm Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: Hey, no shame in preferring NTE or the manga over the show. Different people like different things.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Oct 01, 2014 5:40 pm NTE is great. It’s a nice piece of story-telling told efficiently through some of the better cinematic choices seen in modern filmmaking. The effects are amazing, the setting is wonderful, the mystery is well-earned, and the characters are believable. It’s not a bad series really. NGE is groundbreaking. It’s a very progressive piece of narrative fiction exploring with and through the potentials of the media of film and animation. The effects are well earned, the setting is brilliant, the mystery is gut-wrenching, and the characters are extremely compelling. It’s one of those TV shows that helped pop-culture Japan (and other parts of the world) accept weird filmmaking tactics in its mainstream entertainment, which isn’t an easy task in the slightest.

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Wed Oct 01, 2014 5:48 pm Well I got into the orignal series because of The Rebuilds. Originally I preferred the Rebuilds. Then 3.0 happened, and now I'm terribly conflicted.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Oct 01, 2014 5:52 pm Ray wrote: Then 3.0 happened, and now I'm terribly conflicted.

[]:             Quote Post by KingXanaduu » Wed Oct 01, 2014 5:53 pm Ray wrote: Well I got into the orignal series because of The Rebuilds. Originally I preferred the Rebuilds. Then 3.0 happened, and now I'm terribly conflicted.

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Wed Oct 01, 2014 7:25 pm NGE and EOE are the Anime equivalent of a Greek Tragedy. Shinji Ikari is the modern day tragic hero in the mold of Oedipus. It's great and influential and excellent and informs us of some inherent truth about human nature. But I'm tired of tragedy . Tired of things always being 'dark and grim', and hope being a rare and depleting commodity in settings like Eva (this also extends to my opinion of things like the writings of Lovecraft, and to a lesser extent the majority of the Batman Mythos). Real life isn't always dark and grim and eternally full of despair, there's hope at the end of it all. So why do we as writers/artists/audience tend to dwell so much on the dark? I'm not asking Eva to stop having conflict or angst. I'm just tired of the conflict always ending with everyone (especially Shinji) being worse off for it. EOE has a sliver of hope at the end of it all, but its buried under so much despair, blood and angst I doubt that sliver of hope really would really do any good. 3.0 has much the same issue. But again waiting anxiously for Final before judging. Until then? I don't really have a 'preferred' version of NGE. Why? Because there still hasn't been an Ironclad 'canon' happy ending for Shinji and Company that wasn't hampered by budget concerns and nebulous symbolism (NGE), the authors mental breakdown, and 'tell don't show' storytelling (EOE) or poor writing on the part of another (Sadamoto's Manga). Final is the last hope for any kind of Canon happy ending, it has a lot to deliver given everything 3.0 did to the characters and setting. As a whole though, the franchise adds up to something really endearing, I love it although if you asked me i couldn't explain why.
